item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ALL THE IDAHO PEOPLE WHO GO TO SILVERWOOD (the park that the rollercoaster is from) PUT YOUR HANDS UP!

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The third image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0) as, while the link to the original source has 404'd, the artist still has their page up and can be found here. All of the photos are All Rights Reserved, and it can only be 8implied that the image used here is All Rights Reserved as well. Thus, I have taken down the third image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If a subject is placed within one of the carts of SCP-
2183,
(hereby designated SCP-2183-1), it will deviate
significantly from other carts after traveling a length of
approximately 2,000 m.1

Addendum 2183-03: A subject (D-93730) was nominated
to ride SCP-2183. During the test, all carts traveled as
expected. However, the cart that D-93730 was in detached
from the other carts in midair. The cart (hereby
designated
SCP-2183-1) took a sharp right turn

(hereby designated SCP-2183-1) doesn't need to appear twice in the article

item.title: Track layout April Fool's?
item.content:encoded: Did you change the image for the track layout on April Fool's Day because I think you need to change it back…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's boring at first, but it gets better and the creepiness of what happens to the D-class mashed this really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are some minor tonal and grammatical issues here.

A minimum of four Security Guards are to be assigned around the perimeter.

Security Guard is a non-specific title, it shouldn't be capitalized.

A subject (D-93730) was nominated to ride SCP-2183.

Nominated sounds like an unusual word choice for a d-class task. "Selected"? "Assigned"?

During the test, all carts traveled as expected. However, the cart that D-93730 was in detached from the other carts in midair.

Is the first sentence referring to the same test? It sounds like you meant to say something like "All carts traveled as per the behavior observed in unoccupied tests, with the exception of the cart on which D-93730 rode. The occupied cart…" As it is you say that all the carts traveled as normal, then seem to counter yourself in the next sentence.

Efforts were focused on stopping the cart from reaching the town, and retrieving D-93730. Over the course of several weeks, local reports of SCP-2183-1 sightings appeared in the neighboring towns. … D-93730 was approved for rescue via aerial lift.

The structure of the writing seems odd here. You say that efforts are focused on retrieving the D-Class, move into how he is trapped for several weeks, then return to describing those efforts involving the rocket, etc. It would make more sense to say that efforts began, describe those efforts, then after that move into the after effects - trapped for weeks, yelling all the time, amnestics, etc.

(presumably from repeated missile strikes)

You describe the effect of multiple missile strikes on the cart itself but only mention one missile strike in the story itself. Parenthetical phrases are also not fitting to the tone of an article.

D-93730 (SCP-2183-2) was found suspended in air 124 m above SCP-2183-1, in a seated position. D-93730's arms are at his sides, and his expression indicates distress.

This needs something on when he was found suspended. The previous parts of the story take place over years. Did he reappear floating in the air immediately after his death from the missile? Was it only after the crash of the cart? You also have a bit of a tense misalignment - "found suspended" indicates past tense but "indicates" is present. I see how this could be correct, as the article implies he would float there forever but I think "indicating distress" aligns better and makes for smoother reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My goodness, that image is from Silverwood Theme Park! I know there.

Otherwise, it's okay. A lot of whatever. Totally fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the love of me I cannot stop hearing the goddamn Benny Hill theme tune whenever I read this.

Also, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That poor D-Class fellow is probably still riding somewhere…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how even though the censor bar is there just to cover up the sign in the original photo, it makes the SCP more spooky than it looks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to get off Mr. Bones Wild Ride!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's haunted. Also, hitting a roller coaster car with missiles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what exactly is going on here aside from Mr. Bones' Wild Ride?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for making Mr. Bones' Wild Ride into an amazing skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh! I remember the older version of this one, where the mystery of the coaster was sidestepped in favor for some (IMO confusing) narrative regarding the engineer who was trying to contain it, but this is way better. Kudos on taking the cool bits and re-spinning 'em to something cooler!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 because I really really like this one. At first I thought it was similar to SCP-112, how the track disappeared and everything. It's very different, though, and I love the addendum. That really added a lot, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ride never ends…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, not least for setting up a scenario that does not break my suspension of disbelief in which multiple guided missiles are fired on a rogue flying roller coaster cart. That is some very high-quality weirdness there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably the same one, as per the image credit link.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
" with a speed of approximately 200km."

Did you mean 200 km/hr?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's rather peculiar, because the fact the roller coaster in the image is right by the road and is an old wooden track coaster reminds me of a real park that exists in Idaho.
Coincidence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I want to upvote Mr. Bones wild ride!"

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "what the crap is happening to that poor D-class guy" question is what makes this work. Excellent weirdness. +1

item.title: Re: Author Note
item.content:encoded: Aha, sorry Jarbatalapus. You're included too. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea of a roller coaster car with a mind of its own, and a poor D-Class along for the ride. Absolutely a +1

item.title: Re: Author Note
item.content:encoded:
is secretly sad that my name didn't get mentioned
Nice job, man!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good. +1

item.title: Re: Author Note
item.content:encoded: I liked this in chat, and I like it now. +1 from me.

item.title: Author Note
item.content:encoded:
Hey guys. I decided to re-write SCP-2183 because it was bringing down my average rating I wasn't satisfied with its current state. This time, I opted for a focus on the roller coaster carts.

Thanks to AndarielHalo, TwistedGears, Decibelle, Cimmerian, Blue6D, JamJam66, Dr Leonard, and everyone else who looked at my draft.

CC:

1st image (of the coaster) by Rober Ashworth, CC: https://www.flickr.com/photos/theslowlane/1771734492/

2nd image (of the cart) by Keoni Cabral, CC: https://www.flickr.com/photos/keoni101/5541527795

The layout image is made by me.

NOTE: If you haven't already, make sure to check out the companion tale, The Ride Never Ends
